libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For version 2.0.0, migrate tests to the jakarta.activation package; do not keep javax.activation imports from the existing 1.2.x tests. Keep assertions focused on the public Jakarta Activation API behavior rather than com.sun.activation internals.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides the Jakarta Activation implementation classes and META-INF/mailcap.default and META-INF/mimetypes.default resources needed by normal Activation API runtime paths; the jakarta.activation-api POM explicitly describes the artifact as compile-only and excludes implementation-specific classes.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The implementation artifact contains duplicate jakarta.activation API classes as well as the missing implementation classes; local Gradle and Native Image verification must confirm classpath ordering works as expected.
Some API-only paths can compile and run without the implementation, but tests that exercise normal MIME type and mailcap command-map behavior will fail at runtime without the implementation classes.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jakarta Activation API 2.0.0 is an API-only artifact with no Maven runtime dependencies; core normal-use classes such as MimetypesFileTypeMap and MailcapCommandMap reference com.sun.activation.registries classes and default resources that are not present in the API jar, so meaningful runtime/native coverage should add the matching implementation artifact.
